sn12.65:1.2: “Before my awakening, *bhikkhus*, while I was still just a bodhisatta, not yet fully awakened, this thought occurred to me:
sn12.65:1.3: ‘This world has truly fallen into difficulty: it is born, it ages, it dies, it passes away, and it is reborn.
sn12.65:1.4: And yet it does not understand the escape from this *dukkha*, from aging and death.
sn12.65:1.5: When will the escape from this *dukkha*, from aging and death, become apparent?’
sn12.65:1.7: ‘When what exists is there aging and death? Because of what is there aging and death?’
sn12.65:1.8: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:1.9: ‘When there is birth, there is aging and death; because of birth, there is aging and death.’
sn12.65:2.11: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:2.12: ‘When there is consciousness [*viññāṇa*], there is *nāmarūpa*; because of consciousness [*viññāṇa*], there is *nāmarūpa*.’
sn12.65:2.14: ‘When what exists is there consciousness [*viññāṇa*]? Because of what is there consciousness [*viññāṇa*]?’
sn12.65:2.15: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:2.16: ‘When there is *nāmarūpa*, there is consciousness [*viññāṇa*]; because of *nāmarūpa*, there is consciousness [*viññāṇa*].’
sn12.65:2.8: there is dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] …
sn12.65:3.2: this consciousness [*viññāṇa*] turns back from *nāmarūpa*; it does not go further.
sn12.65:3.3: Only to this extent is there being born, or aging, or dying, or passing away, or being reborn: namely, with *nāmarūpa* as condition, consciousness [*viññāṇa*];
sn12.65:3.5: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*];
sn12.65:3.6: with dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact …
sn12.65:3.7: thus there is the arising of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
sn12.65:3.8: ‘Origination, origination,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, the eye arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowing arose, light arose.
sn12.65:4.14: there is no dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] …
sn12.65:4.16: Through the cessation of what is there the cessation of *nāmarūpa*?’
sn12.65:4.17: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:4.18: ‘When there is no consciousness [*viññāṇa*], there is no *nāmarūpa*;
sn12.65:4.19: through the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*] there is the cessation of *nāmarūpa*.’
sn12.65:4.2: ‘When what does not exist is there no aging and death?
sn12.65:4.3: Through the cessation of what is there the cessation of aging and death?’
sn12.65:4.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:4.5: ‘When there is no birth, there is no aging and death;
sn12.65:4.6: through the cessation of birth there is the cessation of aging and death.’
sn12.65:4.8: ‘When what does not exist is there no birth? …
sn12.65:5.2: ‘When what does not exist is there no consciousness [*viññāṇa*]?
sn12.65:5.3: Through the cessation of what is there the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*]?’
sn12.65:5.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, through appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], there came to me a breakthrough by discernment [*paññā*]:
sn12.65:5.5: ‘When there is no *nāmarūpa*, there is no consciousness [*viññāṇa*];
sn12.65:5.6: through the cessation of *nāmarūpa* there is the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*].’
sn12.65:6.2: I have attained this path to awakening, that is:
sn12.65:6.3: through the cessation of *nāmarūpa* there is the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*];
sn12.65:6.4: through the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*] there is the cessation of *nāmarūpa*;
sn12.65:6.5: through the cessation of *nāmarūpa* there is the cessation of dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.65:6.6: through the cessation of dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] there is the cessation of contact …
sn12.65:6.7: thus there is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
sn12.65:6.8: ‘Cessation, cessation,’ *bhikkhus*—regarding *dhammas* not previously heard, the eye arose in me, knowledge arose, discernment [*paññā*] arose, true knowing arose, light arose.
sn12.65:7.1: Suppose, *bhikkhus*, a man wandering in a forest wilderness were to see an ancient road, an ancient path, followed by people of former times.
sn12.65:7.10: Just so, *bhikkhus*, I saw an ancient road, an ancient path, followed by perfectly awakened ones of former times.
sn12.65:7.3: Following it, he would see an ancient city, an ancient royal capital, inhabited by people of former times, endowed with parks, endowed with groves, endowed with ponds, with embankments, delightful.
sn12.65:7.4: Then, *bhikkhus*, that man would report to the king or to a royal minister:
sn12.65:7.5: ‘Please, *bhante*, know this: while wandering in a forest wilderness I saw an ancient road, an ancient path, followed by people of former times, and I followed it.
sn12.65:7.6: Following it, I saw an ancient city, an ancient royal capital, inhabited by people of former times, endowed with parks, endowed with groves, endowed with ponds, with embankments, delightful.
sn12.65:7.8: Then, *bhikkhus*, that king or royal minister would have that city rebuilt.
sn12.65:7.9: And after some time that city would become prosperous and flourishing, populous, crowded with people, and having attained growth and expansion.
sn12.65:8.1: And what, *bhikkhus*, is that ancient road, that ancient path, followed by perfectly awakened ones of former times?
sn12.65:8.10: following it, I directly knew birth …
sn12.65:8.16: I directly knew dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanaṁ*] …
sn12.65:8.2: It is just this noble eightfold path, that is to say—
sn12.65:8.21: I directly knew the origination of *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.65:8.22: I directly knew the cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
sn12.65:8.23: I directly knew the way of practice leading to the cessation of *saṅkhāras*.
sn12.65:8.24: Having directly known that, I taught it to *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, male lay followers, and female lay followers.
sn12.65:8.25: And so, *bhikkhus*, this holy life has become prosperous and flourishing, widespread, well known among many people, extensive, and well proclaimed among devas and humans.”
sn12.65:8.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that ancient road, that ancient path, followed by perfectly awakened ones of former times. I followed it;
sn12.65:8.5: following it, I directly knew aging and death;
sn12.65:8.6: I directly knew the origination of aging and death;
sn12.65:8.7: I directly knew the cessation of aging and death;
sn12.65:8.8: I directly knew the way of practice leading to the cessation of aging and death.
